what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in which Tim records a short point about heading scoping and block containment, agreeing with an earlier comment and suggesting the underlying logic could be generalized into a reusable registry for block-based structures.
observations: The item is a note authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel, sourced from claude_code, state posted.
It links to item-043cb37b via a commented_on relation.
The body quotes a prior explanation of heading scoping in Markdown/HTML and the need to synthesize containment from a real parser AST.
Tim adds that the same containment graph/tree logic applies beyond Markdown/HTML and could live in a reusable registry.
standouts: Proposal to abstract heading/containment logic into a generic, reusable block-tree registry rather than treating it as format-specific.
Emphasis on building containment from a real AST (CommonMark/remark/mdast or DOM) and storing computed subtree boundaries instead of recomputing heuristically.
Explicit agreement with and extension of the prior item-043cb37b.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter including id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a [point] marker and inline quotation of the prior comment.
History records a single filed transition.
concerns: The note is informal and mixes agreement with a design proposal without concrete implementation details or examples.
It references the prior item-043cb37b without including its content, so full context is not self-contained.
notes: Tim's note agreeing that heading containment should be derived from a real AST and proposing the block-tree logic be made a reusable registry abstraction.
questions: What is the intended scope and interface of the proposed reusable registry?
Which other substrate types besides Markdown/HTML are expected to use this block-containment logic?
How does this proposal relate structurally to the content of item-043cb37b?
Is there an existing registry design this note is meant to extend?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (a posted point/note) matches the content: design commentary that agrees on a heading-scoping approach and proposes a reusable block-containment abstraction.
intention_signals: Capture consensus on the heading-scoping problem and elevate the solution into a generic, reusable block-containment primitive applicable to multiple substrate types.
novelty: Treats heading-scope containment as a format-agnostic block-tree concern and suggests registering it as a reusable component rather than a one-off Markdown/HTML heuristic.
contribution: Records the insight that block containment logic can be decoupled from specific formats and reused via a registry, while keeping the emphasis on real AST-based boundary computation.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on heading scoping, advocating AST-based subtree boundary computation and proposing the underlying block-containment logic as a reusable registry abstraction.
intention_for_embedding: To capture agreement on a robust heading/containment parsing approach and propose generalizing it into a reusable block-tree registry.
